options: Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration, which is for inviting external users to sign in with their home tenant credentials and does not provide silent SSO for internal employees.
Microsoft Entra seamless single sign-on (Seamless SSO), which automatically signs domain-joined users into Microsoft 365 and other Entra apps without re-prompting on the network.
Microsoft Entra password protection for AD, which prevents users from setting weak or banned passwords in on-premises AD but has no effect on browser sign-in for Microsoft 365.
Microsoft Entra Application Proxy, which publishes on-premises web apps externally through Entra ID and is used for legacy intranet apps, not for SSO into Microsoft 365 itself.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because B2B collaboration is for external user invitations, not internal-user silent SSO. Option B is correct because Microsoft Entra seamless SSO is the feature configured via Entra Connect that lets domain-joined users on the corporate network sign in to Microsoft 365 silently using Kerberos. Option C is wrong because password protection enforces banned-password lists in AD and does not affect SSO. Option D is wrong because Application Proxy publishes on-premises web apps externally and is not the SSO mechanism for Microsoft 365.

questions.question: The CIO wants to ensure that even after a user signs in successfully, their browser session is forced to re-authenticate every four hours for high-value SaaS apps. Which Conditional Access capability supports this requirement?
options: A grant control that requires MFA on each sign-in, which will challenge with MFA on every authentication but does not specifically force a fixed re-authentication interval.
A user-risk policy in Microsoft Entra ID Protection that forces a password change when the user reaches medium or high risk, unrelated to imposing a fixed browser session lifetime.
A device platform condition that excludes mobile devices and requires Windows or macOS, which scopes who the policy applies to but does not control session re-evaluation interval.
A session control that sets a sign-in frequency of four hours for the targeted apps, prompting Microsoft Entra ID to re-authenticate the user at that interval regardless of token.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because requiring MFA on each sign-in does not directly control the lifetime of the browser session or refresh token. Option B is wrong because user-risk policies trigger remediation when risk changes, not a fixed session lifetime. Option C is wrong because device platform conditions only limit which devices the policy applies to, not session duration. Option D is correct because the sign-in frequency session control is exactly the mechanism that forces re-authentication at the configured interval for targeted apps.

questions.question: A user reports that Microsoft Entra ID Protection detected an atypical travel sign-in event from their account, showing two sign-ins from countries that are physically impossible to travel between in the given time. Which risk category does this match?
options: A user-risk-only detection that does not influence the sign-in risk score and is therefore evaluated only by user-risk policies that force remediation such as a password reset.
A leaked-credentials user-risk detection, raised when Microsoft's threat intelligence finds the user's credentials posted publicly on the dark web, not from geographic patterns.
A sign-in risk detection in Microsoft Entra ID Protection, which flags the specific event because the distance and time between sign-ins are not physically plausible.
A Conditional Access named-location violation, raised any time a user signs in from outside the configured trusted IP ranges regardless of whether the behavior itself is risky.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because atypical travel is classified as a sign-in risk detection, not a user-risk-only signal. Option B is wrong because leaked credentials is a separate user-risk detection driven by threat intelligence and is unrelated to geographic patterns. Option C is correct because atypical travel is one of Identity Protection's classic sign-in risk detections, flagging an event whose geography and timing are not physically feasible. Option D is wrong because named-location violations are unrelated to atypical travel detection.

questions.question: Which statement most accurately describes the difference between Microsoft Entra security groups and Microsoft 365 groups?
options: Microsoft 365 groups can be the target for built-in Microsoft Entra directory roles, while security groups are limited to grouping users for Exchange distribution lists only.
Security groups manage access to resources for users and devices, while Microsoft 365 groups provide collaboration resources like a shared mailbox, calendar, and SharePoint site.
Security groups can only contain devices and service principals, while Microsoft 365 groups are the only type that can grant access to Azure resources or assign directory roles.
Security groups are deprecated in modern Microsoft Entra ID and have been replaced by administrative units, while Microsoft 365 groups remain the only way to grant resource access.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because role-assignable groups are typically security groups (with the role-assignable flag), not Microsoft 365 groups. Option B is correct because security groups are the general-purpose grouping mechanism for access management of users and devices, while Microsoft 365 groups are designed for collaboration and include a shared mailbox, calendar, and SharePoint site. Option C is wrong because security groups can contain users, devices, service principals, and other groups. Option D is wrong because security groups are not deprecated.

questions.question: An administrator wants users to register security information for self-service password reset and MFA from a single combined portal experience. Which Microsoft Entra feature provides this unified registration experience?
options: The legacy Microsoft Entra MFA per-user registration prompt, which appears at next sign-in for users enabled for per-user MFA but collects MFA methods only, not SSPR methods.
Microsoft Entra ID Governance entitlement management access packages, which let administrators bundle resource access for a project but do not collect authentication methods.
Microsoft Entra Verified ID, which issues and verifies digital credentials such as employee badges and certifications but is not the mechanism for capturing MFA/SSPR registration.
The combined security information registration experience in Microsoft Entra ID, which lets users register methods such as Authenticator, phone, and email once for MFA and SSPR.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because the legacy per-user MFA registration covers only MFA methods, not SSPR registration. Option B is wrong because entitlement management bundles resource access; it does not capture authentication methods. Option C is wrong because Microsoft Entra Verified ID is for issuing and verifying digital credentials, not for capturing MFA/SSPR registration. Option D is correct because the combined security information registration experience lets users register authentication methods once for both MFA and SSPR.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best example of a workload identity in Microsoft Entra ID rather than a user identity?
options: A managed identity assigned to an Azure App Service web app, managed automatically by Microsoft Entra ID and used to authenticate to Key Vault or Storage with no code credentials.
A member user account named svc-backups@contoso.com that an admin created and shares among the backup team, which is technically a human-style user identity used for automation.
An external guest user invited from a partner tenant granted access to a SharePoint site as part of a Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration for joint project document review.
A device object registered in Microsoft Entra ID when a Windows 11 laptop is Entra-joined, representing the device but administered separately from the service principal model.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because a managed identity is a quintessential workload identity in Microsoft Entra ID; it represents an Azure resource (the App Service app) and is used for app-to-service authentication. Option B is wrong because a shared service account represented as a user object is still a (discouraged) user identity, not a modern workload identity. Option C is wrong because a guest user is a human external collaborator. Option D is wrong because a device object represents a device, not a workload that authenticates as an application.

questions.question: A help-desk technician needs to reset passwords for all non-administrator users in the tenant but should not be able to reset passwords for users who hold privileged roles. Which built-in Microsoft Entra role most closely satisfies this requirement out of the box?
options: User Administrator, which manages all users including resetting passwords for administrators, making it too broad for a help desk that should not touch admin accounts.
Authentication Administrator, which manages auth methods and resets passwords; Privileged Authentication Administrator is required to reset passwords for users in admin roles.
Helpdesk Administrator, which can reset passwords for non-administrator users and users in certain limited roles but cannot reset passwords for users in privileged admin roles.
Global Administrator, which has full directory control and can reset any user's password including admins, far more access than the requirement and a least-privilege issue.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because User Administrator can reset passwords for any user including administrators, which exceeds the requirement. Option B is wrong because Authentication Administrator focuses on managing methods broadly; the canonical help-desk role with the described scope is Helpdesk Administrator. Option C is correct because Helpdesk Administrator is documented as able to reset passwords for non-administrator users while being restricted from resetting passwords on privileged role members. Option D is wrong because Global Administrator is far too broad.

questions.question: Which authentication method in Microsoft Entra ID is considered the most phishing-resistant for everyday user sign-in?
options: FIDO2 security keys, which use public-key cryptography bound to the relying-party origin so the credential cannot be replayed against a phishing site.
SMS-based one-time codes delivered to the user's mobile phone, which are simple to deploy but vulnerable to SIM swap and adversary-in-the-middle phishing of the code.
Security questions configured during SSPR registration, easy for users but giving weak assurance because the answers are often guessable or visible on social media.
Voice call to a registered office or mobile phone number, which the user answers and presses # to approve, but offers limited resistance to call-forwarding attacks and is weak.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because FIDO2 security keys are explicitly recognized by Microsoft as phishing-resistant because the credential is bound to the relying-party origin and cannot be replayed against a fake site. Option B is wrong because SMS is vulnerable to SIM swap and adversary-in-the-middle attacks and is not phishing-resistant. Option C is wrong because security questions are a weak factor and not phishing-resistant. Option D is wrong because voice calls are weak and susceptible to call-forwarding attacks.

questions.question: An organization wants any application registered in Microsoft Entra ID to be reviewed and approved by an administrator before it can be granted tenant-wide permissions to access organizational data. Which feature most directly supports this requirement?
options: The admin consent workflow in Microsoft Entra ID, which routes user consent requests to designated reviewers so that tenant-wide permissions are only granted after admin approval.
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management for Azure resource roles, which provides JIT elevation and approval for Azure RBAC roles but not for application consent flows.
Conditional Access app control via Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps reverse-proxy session policies, which inspect in-session traffic but do not gate the initial consent flow.
Microsoft Entra access reviews for application assignments, which periodically have reviewers attest who is assigned to an app but do not prevent the initial grant of permissions.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because the admin consent workflow is specifically designed to require administrator approval before broad/tenant-wide permissions are granted to applications. Option B is wrong because PIM for Azure resource roles is about RBAC role activation, not app consent. Option C is wrong because Defender for Cloud Apps session policies act on live sessions, not on consent. Option D is wrong because access reviews periodically reattest assignments and do not gate the initial consent grant.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Entra ID feature is specifically intended to allow guest users from partner organizations to sign in to Contoso's resources using credentials managed by their own home tenant?
options: Microsoft Entra Connect with pass-through authentication, which validates passwords against Contoso's on-premises Active Directory but is for Contoso's own employees, not partners.
Microsoft Entra Verified ID, which lets organizations issue and verify digital credentials such as proof of employment but is not a sign-in method for partner users into apps.
Microsoft Entra External ID (B2B collaboration), which lets Contoso invite users from partner tenants or social identity providers while credentials stay in their home tenant.
Microsoft Entra Domain Services, which provides managed AD such as LDAP, Kerberos, and domain join for legacy apps but is not used for partner-user authentication scenarios.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because pass-through authentication validates passwords against on-premises AD for Contoso's own employees, not partner users. Option B is wrong because Verified ID is about issuing/verifying digital credentials, not interactive partner sign-in. Option C is correct because Microsoft Entra External ID (B2B collaboration) is the feature that lets external partner users sign in to Contoso's resources using their home-tenant credentials. Option D is wrong because Microsoft Entra Domain Services provides managed AD DS for legacy apps and is not for partner sign-in.

questions.question: A security administrator is reviewing Microsoft Entra Conditional Access access controls. Which of the following is an example of a session control rather than a grant control?
options: Require multifactor authentication before granting access to the targeted cloud application, which prompts the user with an MFA challenge as part of the sign-in evaluation.
Require a device to be marked as compliant in Microsoft Intune or to be hybrid Azure AD joined, which is enforced at the moment Microsoft Entra ID evaluates the sign-in request.
Require an approved client app such as Microsoft Outlook for iOS or Android, which restricts which mobile apps can connect to Microsoft 365 after Conditional Access evaluation.
Use app-enforced restrictions to limit SharePoint Online downloads to view-only on unmanaged devices, which is applied during the session rather than as a check during sign-in.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because requiring MFA is a grant control evaluated at the time of authentication. Option B is wrong because requiring a compliant or hybrid-joined device is also a grant control. Option C is wrong because requiring an approved client app is a grant control restricting which client can obtain a token. Option D is correct because app-enforced restrictions (such as SharePoint Online view-only for unmanaged devices) are session controls that shape behavior during the session itself.

questions.question: A user's Microsoft 365 sign-in is being repeatedly blocked. The Microsoft Entra sign-in logs show the user has reached a high user-risk state in Microsoft Entra ID Protection. What is the most likely remediation that a user-risk-based Conditional Access policy will require?
options: Block sign-in entirely with no remediation path, requiring the user to contact the help desk who must manually re-enable the account before any further sign-in attempts.
Require the user to register an additional method such as a FIDO2 security key before any further sign-in is allowed, regardless of whether existing credentials still work.
Require a secure password change so the user must change their password using strong authentication (typically MFA), which resets the user's risk to none on successful completion.
Force the user to delete and re-create their account in the Microsoft Entra admin center, since high user risk is treated as a permanent compromise that cannot be remediated.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because user-risk-based policies typically provide a remediation path, not a permanent block. Option B is wrong because registering an additional method is not the standard user-risk remediation; password reset is. Option C is correct because the canonical user-risk remediation in Identity Protection is to require a secure password change that, upon MFA-backed completion, resets the user's risk state. Option D is wrong because user accounts are never required to be deleted as a remediation step.

questions.question: Which statement best describes a named location in Microsoft Entra Conditional Access?
options: A named location is a Microsoft Entra group used to assign policies to users in a specific country, with membership automatically maintained based on the user's mailing address.
A named location is the same as a security group in Microsoft Entra ID and can be the assignment target for both Conditional Access policies and Microsoft Entra role assignments.
A named location is a label attached to a device object in Microsoft Entra ID describing where the device is currently located, used by Conditional Access to scope policies.
A named location is a defined IPv4/IPv6 range or set of countries used as a condition in Conditional Access policies to require, exclude, or evaluate trust based on network origin.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because named locations are not groups and are not driven by user mailing addresses. Option B is wrong because named locations are network constructs, not user groups, and are not used to assign directory roles. Option C is wrong because named locations are not labels on device objects; they describe networks. Option D is correct because Microsoft Entra named locations are IP ranges or country/region sets that Conditional Access uses to make decisions based on network origin.

questions.question: An administrator wants to ensure that all sign-ins to high-risk SaaS applications happen only from devices that the organization manages. Which Conditional Access grant control most directly satisfies this requirement?
options: Require device to be marked as compliant (Intune-managed) or hybrid Microsoft Entra joined, ensuring only organization-managed devices pass the policy.
Require Microsoft Entra ID P2 Privileged Identity Management activation for the target SaaS app before sign-in is allowed, so access becomes a just-in-time, audited operation.
Require an approved client app such as Microsoft Edge or Outlook for mobile, which restricts the family of apps a user may use but does not prove the device itself is managed.
Require terms-of-use acceptance through Microsoft Entra terms of use, which ensures users read a legal notice but does not enforce that the connecting device is managed.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because the require-compliant-device-or-hybrid-Azure-AD-joined grant control is the standard way to require that the connecting device is managed by the organization. Option B is wrong because PIM is for role activation, not for end-user SaaS sign-in flows. Option C is wrong because the approved-client-app control restricts the app, not the device's management state. Option D is wrong because terms of use only enforces acknowledgment of a notice, not the device's compliance state.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best example of using Microsoft Entra ID's built-in role-based access control (RBAC) for directory roles rather than Azure resource RBAC?
options: Granting a user the Owner role on an Azure resource group via the Access control (IAM) blade so they can create virtual machines, storage accounts, and other resources in scope.
Granting a user the Contributor role on an Azure subscription via Azure RBAC so they can create and manage all resources except for assigning roles to identities at that level.
Granting a user the Reader role on a virtual network in Azure via Azure RBAC so they can view the virtual network and its subnets but cannot make any changes to it.
Granting a user the User Administrator role from Roles and administrators in the Microsoft Entra admin center so they can create users, reset passwords, and manage groups.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because the Owner role on a resource group is Azure resource RBAC, not Microsoft Entra directory RBAC. Option B is wrong because the Contributor role on a subscription is also Azure resource RBAC. Option C is wrong because Reader on a virtual network is again Azure resource RBAC. Option D is correct because User Administrator is a Microsoft Entra directory role assigned in the Entra admin center, the directory-RBAC plane covering identity-management permissions.

questions.question: Which scenario most clearly justifies using Microsoft Entra ID custom roles instead of one of the built-in directory roles?
options: Granting a user the ability to manage every Microsoft 365 service in the tenant, such as Exchange, SharePoint, and Teams, which is already covered by built-in Global Admin.
Granting a user permission to reset passwords for all users in the tenant, since this is precisely what the built-in User Administrator and Helpdesk Administrator roles already do.
Granting a user the ability to read all users and groups in the directory, which is the scope of the built-in Directory Readers and Global Reader roles and needs no custom role.
Granting a user the ability to update only a specific extension attribute on user objects with no other permissions, which no single built-in role provides at that granularity.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Global Administrator already covers all Microsoft 365 service administration. Option B is wrong because User Administrator or Helpdesk Administrator already covers password-reset scenarios. Option C is wrong because Directory Readers / Global Reader already provide directory read access. Option D is correct because custom roles exist exactly for granular permissions that no single built-in role provides, such as updating a specific user attribute without any other rights.

questions.question: A security team wants to retire legacy authentication (POP3, IMAP, SMTP AUTH, older Office clients) for accessing Exchange Online. Which Microsoft Entra capability most directly enables blocking legacy authentication across all users?
options: A Conditional Access policy targeting All users and All cloud apps with a client apps condition for legacy authentication and a Block access grant control to deny those sessions.
Microsoft Entra Identity Protection user-risk policy with the action set to block sign-in, which only acts on accounts that already reached elevated risk, not on legacy protocols.
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management with permanent assignment removal, which reduces standing admin access rather than restricting authentication protocols.
Microsoft Entra access reviews configured on Microsoft 365 groups, which periodically have reviewers attest that members still need access but cannot enforce protocol restrictions.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because a Conditional Access policy with the client apps condition narrowed to legacy clients and a Block access grant control is the standard, recommended way to retire legacy authentication tenant-wide. Option B is wrong because Identity Protection user-risk policies act on user risk, not on protocol selection. Option C is wrong because PIM is about role assignments and JIT elevation, not protocol blocking. Option D is wrong because access reviews reattest membership.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the role of Microsoft Entra Connect in a hybrid identity deployment?
options: Microsoft Entra Connect is an on-premises sync tool that integrates on-premises Active Directory with Microsoft Entra ID, supporting sign-in options like PHS, PTA, and federation.
Microsoft Entra Connect is a cloud-only service that replaces on-premises Active Directory by migrating users, groups, and devices into Microsoft Entra ID and retiring AD DS.
Microsoft Entra Connect is a Conditional Access policy template in Microsoft Entra ID that combines MFA enforcement, device compliance, and risk-based controls into one policy.
Microsoft Entra Connect is a Microsoft 365 Defender component that protects identities by monitoring domain controllers for activity such as DCSync attacks and golden tickets.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because Microsoft Entra Connect is the on-premises synchronization tool that integrates AD DS with Microsoft Entra ID and supports sign-in options such as password hash sync, pass-through authentication, and federation. Option B is wrong because Entra Connect synchronizes with on-premises AD; it does not replace it. Option C is wrong because Entra Connect is not a Conditional Access policy template. Option D is wrong because monitoring DCs for attacks is a Microsoft Defender for Identity capability.

questions.question: An administrator wants users to sign in to Windows 11 laptops using a biometric gesture (face or fingerprint) or a PIN tied to the device's TPM, rather than a password. Which Microsoft Entra-compatible authentication method matches this description?
options: Microsoft Authenticator app passwordless phone sign-in, which delivers a push notification with number matching to a mobile device to approve sign-in without entering a password.
Windows Hello for Business, which provides device-bound passwordless authentication using biometrics or a PIN backed by the device's TPM and is a strong Entra method.
FIDO2 security keys from a vendor such as Yubico or Feitian, which provide passwordless sign-in via a hardware key but require carrying a physical key, not the laptop itself.
Temporary Access Pass (TAP), a time-limited passcode issued by an admin to bootstrap registration of stronger authentication methods such as Microsoft Authenticator or FIDO2 keys.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Authenticator passwordless phone sign-in uses a mobile device, not the laptop's TPM and biometrics. Option B is correct because Windows Hello for Business is exactly the method that provides device-bound passwordless sign-in using biometrics or a PIN backed by the TPM. Option C is wrong because FIDO2 keys are hardware-key based, not bound to the laptop's TPM and biometrics. Option D is wrong because Temporary Access Pass is a bootstrapping passcode for registration, not an everyday sign-in method.

questions.question: Contoso wants to ensure that critical Microsoft Entra directory role assignments such as Global Administrator are not held permanently, but instead activated only when needed and audited. Which Microsoft Entra feature is designed for this just-in-time admin access model?
options: Microsoft Entra access reviews, which periodically have a designated reviewer attest that role assignments and group memberships are still needed but do not enforce JIT activation.
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management (PIM), which lets organizations make role assignments eligible rather than active, requiring JIT activation with approval and audit.
Microsoft Entra terms of use, which lets administrators require users to accept a legal notice before accessing a cloud app but does not control role assignment or activation.
Microsoft Entra Identity Protection sign-in risk policy, which evaluates risky sign-ins in real time and can block or require MFA but is unrelated to making admin assignments JIT.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because access reviews periodically attest assignments but don't provide JIT activation. Option B is correct because Privileged Identity Management is the feature that lets admins be eligible for a role and activate it just-in-time, with approval, MFA, and audit, which is exactly what's described. Option C is wrong because terms of use is about acknowledging a legal notice, not role activation. Option D is wrong because Identity Protection sign-in risk evaluates risk, not role activation workflows.

questions.question: An administrator wants to ensure that during a self-service password reset, users must verify their identity using two different methods (for example, the Microsoft Authenticator app and an email). Which SSPR setting controls this?
options: The registration setting that requires users to register when signing in, which determines when users are prompted to register methods but does not control method count at reset.
The admin role-based password reset policy, a separate stricter policy for admin accounts that forces multiple methods on admins regardless of the user setting in the SSPR blade.
The notification setting that sends an email to the user and to all Global Administrators when an admin's password is reset, which is an audit feature and does not affect methods.
The Number of methods required to reset setting in the SSPR blade, which can be set to one or two methods and determines how many challenges the user must pass during reset.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because the registration setting controls when users register methods, not how many are used during reset. Option B is wrong because while admins have a stricter policy, the requirement here applies to regular users and is controlled by the user-facing SSPR setting. Option C is wrong because the admin notification setting is an audit feature and doesn't change the number of challenges. Option D is correct because the SSPR blade has an explicit Number of methods required to reset setting that determines whether one or two methods must be used.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Entra feature most directly helps reduce the risk of compromised credentials by enforcing banned-password lists and detecting common variations such as P@ssw0rd1 and Summer2024!?
options: Microsoft Entra ID Protection user-risk policy, which forces a password change when leaked credentials are detected but does not stop a user from picking a new banned password.
Microsoft Entra password protection, which applies a global banned-password list from Microsoft plus a custom banned-password list defined by the org to block weak passwords.
Conditional Access password-strength policy, configurable in the Conditional Access blade, where admins define the minimum complexity rules that user passwords must satisfy.
Microsoft Entra ID Governance access reviews, which periodically reattest user access to apps and resources but cannot enforce that users choose strong passwords on a change.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Identity Protection user-risk policy can force a password change but does not enforce a banned-password list at change time. Option B is correct because Microsoft Entra password protection is the feature that enforces the global and custom banned-password lists and is designed to block common weak variants. Option C is wrong because there is no Conditional Access password-strength policy; password complexity is governed by password protection. Option D is wrong because access reviews reattest access, not password strength.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Entra ID license tier is required to use dynamic membership rules for groups, such as automatically including all users whose department equals Sales?
options: Microsoft Entra ID P1, which includes dynamic groups, Conditional Access, self-service password reset with write-back, and other access management features beyond Free.
Microsoft Entra ID Free, which provides basic user and group management including dynamic group membership rules at no extra cost with any Azure or Microsoft 365 subscription.
Microsoft Entra ID Governance, required for any kind of automatic group membership and including lifecycle workflows that move users between dynamic groups as attributes change.
Microsoft Entra External ID, required because dynamic groups can only target external guest users in Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration scenarios and cannot apply to internal users.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because dynamic groups (with attribute-based membership rules) are a Microsoft Entra ID P1 feature. Option B is wrong because Entra ID Free provides basic group management but not dynamic membership. Option C is wrong because Entra ID Governance is an add-on for advanced governance scenarios; dynamic groups themselves are gated by P1. Option D is wrong because dynamic groups apply to member users as well as guests, not only external users.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Entra ID feature is responsible for generating a sign-in risk score in real time during authentication and feeding that signal to Conditional Access for risk-based decisions?
options: Microsoft Entra ID Protection, which evaluates sign-in events for behaviors such as anonymous IP, atypical travel, and unfamiliar properties and feeds risk into CA policies.
Microsoft Entra Connect, which syncs users and credentials between on-premises Active Directory and Microsoft Entra ID but does not generate any risk score for sign-in events.
Microsoft Entra Verified ID, used to issue and verify decentralized digital credentials for scenarios such as employee badges, is not part of the sign-in risk evaluation flow.
Microsoft Entra terms of use, which records the user's acceptance of a legal notice the first time they access a configured cloud app and is not involved in calculating risk.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because Microsoft Entra ID Protection is the component that evaluates sign-in risk in real time and exposes both sign-in risk and user risk signals for Conditional Access risk-based policies. Option B is wrong because Entra Connect synchronizes identities and credentials; it doesn't generate risk scores. Option C is wrong because Verified ID is about decentralized digital credentials and is unrelated to risk evaluation. Option D is wrong because terms of use is a legal-acceptance feature.

questions.question: An administrator wants Finance users to access the Microsoft 365 admin portals only from corporate-managed devices on the corporate network, while blocking access from any other condition. Which combination of Conditional Access conditions and controls is most appropriate?
options: Apply a policy to Finance users targeting all cloud apps with a Block access grant control and no other conditions, which would block Finance from any Microsoft 365 service.
Apply a policy to Finance users targeting Microsoft admin portals, requiring trusted named location and compliant device, and blocking access when those conditions are not met.
Apply a policy to all users targeting Exchange Online only with a require-MFA grant control, which protects email but does not restrict admin portals or scope to Finance users.
Apply a policy to Finance users targeting all cloud apps with a require-MFA control and no device or location conditions, prompting MFA universally but not enforcing device state.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because it blocks Finance from everything, which is far more restrictive than the requirement. Option B is correct because targeting Microsoft admin portals as the cloud app, requiring trusted named location plus compliant device, and blocking when conditions are not met directly implements the stated scenario. Option C is wrong because the policy targets only Exchange and doesn't constrain admin portal access by network or device. Option D is wrong because requiring only MFA doesn't enforce the device or network requirement.

questions.question: Which of the following describes a key difference between per-user MFA and Conditional Access enforced MFA in Microsoft Entra ID?
options: Per-user MFA is included only with Microsoft Entra ID P2, while Conditional Access enforced MFA is available with Entra ID Free, making CA the easier start for small tenants.
Per-user MFA is enabled on each user account and forces MFA on every sign-in, while Conditional Access enforced MFA is policy-based and requires MFA only under specific conditions.
Per-user MFA only applies when a user signs in from an unmanaged device, while Conditional Access enforced MFA applies on every sign-in regardless of conditions and replaces it.
Per-user MFA can be combined with named locations to exclude trusted IPs in modern Entra ID, while Conditional Access enforced MFA does not support named-location exclusions.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because per-user MFA was historically available with basic licensing, and Conditional Access requires P1 or higher. Option B is correct because per-user MFA is a per-account toggle that forces MFA on every sign-in, while Conditional Access provides policy-based MFA scoped by user, app, location, device state, or risk. Option C is wrong because per-user MFA is not conditioned on device state and applies on every sign-in. Option D is wrong because named locations are a Conditional Access concept.

questions.question: An administrator wants users to be able to sign in to a SaaS application using a one-time passcode emailed to their work email address as an additional verification step. Which authentication method in Microsoft Entra ID supports this scenario?
options: Federation through Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS), which delegates sign-in to an on-premises federation server and is unrelated to email OTPs for verification.
Microsoft Entra Domain Services, which provides managed AD DS such as LDAP and Kerberos for legacy applications but is not used to deliver passcodes to a mailbox at sign-in.
Email one-time passcode (OTP) authentication, which Microsoft Entra ID supports as a verification method, including for Entra B2B guest users whose home tenant cannot be reached.
Microsoft Authenticator app push notifications, which deliver a prompt to the user's mobile device approved with number matching but do not deliver an OTP to the email mailbox.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because federation through AD FS is a sign-in protocol delegation pattern, not an email OTP mechanism. Option B is wrong because Microsoft Entra Domain Services provides managed AD DS for legacy apps, not email OTPs. Option C is correct because Microsoft Entra ID supports an email one-time passcode authentication method, frequently used as a fallback for B2B guests whose home directory cannot deliver a token. Option D is wrong because Authenticator push notifications use the mobile app, not email.

questions.question: Which statement most accurately describes Microsoft Entra Conditional Access compared to Microsoft Entra ID security defaults?
options: Security defaults provide granular, policy-based controls (assignments and access controls) and require Microsoft Entra ID P1, while Conditional Access is one-click in Free.
Conditional Access provides granular, policy-based controls (assignments and grant/session) and requires Entra ID P1, while security defaults are a free one-click toggle.
Conditional Access and security defaults are identical under two names; the only difference is that security defaults is the Free term and Conditional Access is the P1/P2 term.
Security defaults can be customized per user, per app, and per device platform from the Entra admin center, while Conditional Access can only be toggled at the tenant level.
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because the description reverses the two features. Option B is correct because Conditional Access is the granular, policy-based engine (with assignments and grant/session controls) and requires at least Entra ID P1, while security defaults is a free one-click baseline of identity protections. Option C is wrong because security defaults and Conditional Access are distinct features with different capabilities. Option D is wrong because security defaults cannot be customized per user/app/platform.

questions.question: A consulting firm wants to share a Microsoft Teams workspace with three external auditors from a partner firm for a one-month engagement. The auditors must sign in with their existing work accounts at their own company rather than receive newly issued credentials. Which Microsoft Entra capability fits this scenario?
options: Microsoft Entra External ID for customers, which creates a separate customer-facing tenant with branded sign-up and sign-in user flows for consumer identities
Microsoft Entra Domain Services, which provides managed group policy, LDAP, and Kerberos for cloud VMs without deploying domain controllers in Azure
Microsoft Entra Connect cloud sync, which syncs on-premises Active Directory users and password hashes into your tenant for hybrid identity sign-in
Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration, which invites guest users from a partner tenant or other IdPs so they sign in with their home credentials to shared resources
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because External ID for customers targets consumer-facing apps with a separate tenant and user flows, not partner collaboration in your tenant. Option B is wrong because Entra Domain Services provides legacy directory protocols for VMs and is unrelated to inviting external partner users. Option C is wrong because cloud sync moves on-premises AD users into your tenant; it does not invite external partners. Option D is correct because B2B collaboration was built to invite external partners as guests using their home credentials.

questions.question: A retail company is launching a public mobile shopping app and wants customers to register and sign in using either an email and password they create, a Google account, or a Facebook account. The customer identities must stay entirely separate from the workforce tenant used by employees. Which Entra offering fits?
options: Use Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration to invite each customer to the workforce tenant as a guest and assign retail app permissions individually using groups and access packages
Use Microsoft Entra ID Protection user-risk policies in the workforce tenant for customers, since risk-based sign-in evaluates external sign-ins from Google or Facebook accounts
Use Microsoft Entra External ID for customers (formerly Azure AD B2C), a dedicated external tenant with customizable user flows and built-in support for social identity providers
Use Microsoft Entra Connect with pass-through authentication to federate customer identities from on-premises Active Directory so they sign in with synchronized credentials
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because B2B collaboration is for partner workforce scenarios, not millions of customer identities mixed into the employee tenant. Option B is wrong because ID Protection evaluates risk for existing identities and is not a customer registration platform. Option C is correct because Entra External ID for customers (Azure AD B2C) is purpose-built for customer-facing apps with a separate tenant, user flows, and social IdPs. Option D is wrong because Entra Connect federates internal workforce identities, not anonymous customer sign-ups.

questions.question: An administrator wants to grant a senior engineer the ability to activate the Global Administrator role only when needed, with activation requiring MFA, a justification, and approval from a security manager. The engineer should not hold the role permanently. Which Microsoft Entra feature supports this?
options: Microsoft Entra Conditional Access, which evaluates signals such as user, device, and location and can require MFA but does not assign or activate directory roles for administrators
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management (PIM), which supports eligible role assignments, just-in-time activation with MFA, justification, approval workflows, and time-bound windows
Microsoft Entra administrative units, which scope role assignments to a subset of users, groups, or devices but do not provide just-in-time activation or approval for role holders
Microsoft Entra access packages in Entitlement Management, which bundle group, app, and SharePoint permissions with a request workflow but do not gate directory role activation
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Conditional Access enforces access conditions during sign-in but does not assign or activate Entra roles. Option B is correct because PIM is designed for just-in-time role activation with MFA-on-activation, justification, and approver workflows. Option C is wrong because administrative units only scope the targets of a role, not the activation flow. Option D is wrong because access packages govern bundled resource access requests, not directory role activation.

questions.question: A platform team must grant a contractor the Virtual Machine Contributor role on a specific Azure subscription, but only between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. on weekdays for the next month, with each activation requiring justification. Which Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management capability supports this?
options: Configure a Conditional Access policy on All Cloud Apps with a session control that limits the contractor's sessions to nine-to-five Monday through Friday for one month
Configure an access review that runs daily at 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. and uses auto-apply outcomes to add and remove the VM Contributor role each business day for the contractor
Configure an Entitlement Management access package that grants the contractor a permanent VM Contributor role and uses separation-of-duties to ensure no overlap with peers
Configure a PIM for Azure resources eligible assignment for VM Contributor with a one-month window, require justification on activation, and set a maximum activation duration
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Conditional Access cannot bound role assignment hours or grant subscription RBAC roles. Option B is wrong because access reviews recertify access periodically; they do not grant role assignments on a schedule. Option C is wrong because Entitlement Management does not assign Azure RBAC roles, and permanent grants contradict the just-in-time requirement. Option D is correct because PIM for Azure resources supports eligible assignments with time-bound windows, justification, and maximum activation duration.

questions.question: A governance lead wants every department manager to confirm, every 90 days, that the people on their team still need membership in a sensitive Microsoft 365 group. If a manager does not respond within 21 days, the user should be removed automatically. Which Microsoft Entra Governance feature fits?
options: Configure a sign-in risk Conditional Access policy targeting members of the group, which forces password reset every 90 days and blocks sign-in if the user is inactive for 21 days
Configure an Entitlement Management access package that includes the group, set the requestor scope to the manager's direct reports, and a 90-day expiration with SoD checks
Configure an access review of the group with 90-day recurrence, set the reviewer to be each user's manager, set a 21-day window, and enable auto-apply to remove unreviewed members
Configure a lifecycle workflow for the mover scenario that runs every 90 days and, if no manager response is detected within 21 days, removes the user and revokes their licenses
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Conditional Access does not recertify group membership or ask managers to confirm access. Option B is wrong because access packages govern initial provisioning of bundled resources, not recurring manager-driven recertification. Option C is correct because access reviews support manager-as-reviewer, recurrence, response windows, and auto-apply to remove unreviewed users. Option D is wrong because lifecycle workflows automate joiner/mover/leaver tasks based on attribute changes, not periodic manager attestations.

questions.question: A business unit wants new employees to automatically receive a welcome email, get added to specific groups, be provisioned a Teams license, and be assigned a manager based on attributes when their account is created in Microsoft Entra. Which Microsoft Entra ID Governance feature is designed for this?
options: Microsoft Entra Identity Protection sign-in risk policies, which evaluate joiner sign-ins from new locations and require extra verification before granting Microsoft 365 access
Microsoft Entra ID Governance lifecycle workflows, which automate joiner, mover, and leaver tasks such as welcome emails, license assignment, and group adds based on attribute changes
Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration cross-tenant access settings, which control inbound and outbound trust between tenants and apply default redemption rules for new external collaborators
Microsoft Entra Connect synchronization rules, which apply attribute mappings between Active Directory and the cloud directory but do not perform onboarding actions like sending emails
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Identity Protection risk policies handle sign-in risk decisions, not onboarding tasks. Option B is correct because lifecycle workflows in Entra ID Governance automate joiner/mover/leaver tasks like sending welcome mail, group adds, and license assignment. Option C is wrong because cross-tenant access settings govern B2B trust, not internal onboarding. Option D is wrong because Entra Connect sync rules only map attributes; they don't run onboarding workflows.

questions.question: An organization wants project managers to self-request a bundle that includes membership in two security groups, access to a SharePoint site, and assignment to an application, with approval routed to the requestor's manager and automatic expiration after 180 days. Which Entra ID Governance feature fits this?
options: Configure Conditional Access with a grant control requiring manager approval before allowing access to the SharePoint site, security groups, and app individually for each project manager
Configure a PIM assignment for each project manager that bundles the groups, app, and SharePoint into a single eligible activation with manager approval and a 180-day expiry window
Configure a lifecycle workflow that triggers on project manager hire date, adds the groups, SharePoint, and app for 180 days, then removes them automatically on the leaver event
Configure an Entitlement Management access package containing the groups, SharePoint site, and application with a policy requiring manager approval and a 180-day assignment expiration
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Conditional Access cannot grant group, SharePoint, or app assignments; it only enforces access conditions at sign-in. Option B is wrong because PIM governs Entra and Azure role activation, not bundled group and SharePoint resource access. Option C is wrong because lifecycle workflows automate attribute-driven joiner/mover/leaver tasks, not user-initiated requests with approval. Option D is correct because access packages bundle groups, sites, and apps with approval policies and expirations exactly as described.

questions.question: A security architect is comparing eligible and active role assignments in Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management. Which statement most accurately describes an eligible assignment?
options: An eligible assignment grants the role permanently to the user, but every sign-in requires MFA and a justification before Entra honors the role's permissions during the session
An eligible assignment requires the user to perform an activation step — possibly with MFA, justification, and approval — to gain the role's permissions, only for a bounded time window
An eligible assignment provides the role only during break-glass scenarios when a tenant-wide outage is declared and Conditional Access is temporarily suspended for emergency admins
An eligible assignment lets the user delegate the role to another administrator at any time without an activation step, while still tracking the delegation in the Entra audit log
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because eligible assignments are not permanent — the user must explicitly activate to use the role. Option B is correct because eligible assignments require a just-in-time activation step (possibly with MFA, justification, and approval) and grant the role for a bounded window. Option C is wrong because eligibility is not tied to break-glass scenarios; emergency accounts are configured separately. Option D is wrong because eligibility is not a delegation mechanism; it is a personal activation model.

questions.question: A compliance officer needs every user — internal employees and external guests — to read and accept a legal disclaimer the first time they sign in to a specific finance application, and to re-accept it every 12 months. Which Microsoft Entra feature, used with Conditional Access, fits?
options: Configure a PIM eligible assignment for the finance application that prompts the user to accept terms on activation and requires re-activation every 12 months for continued access
Configure an Entitlement Management access package for the finance app with a custom approval question asking users to type 'I accept' before access is granted on each renewal
Configure an Entra access review of the finance app that runs every 12 months and uses self-review with a justification field where each reviewer types acceptance of the disclaimer
Configure a Microsoft Entra terms of use document and reference it in a Conditional Access policy targeting the finance app with an annual reacceptance interval for the user
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because PIM governs role activation, not application access prompting with legal terms. Option B is wrong because access packages handle bundled resource provisioning, not in-line legal acceptance at sign-in. Option C is wrong because access reviews are about recertifying access, not prompting users to accept disclaimers. Option D is correct because Entra terms of use, referenced from a Conditional Access policy, displays and requires acceptance with a configurable reacceptance interval.

questions.question: A tenant administrator notices that any user in the tenant — including standard members — can invite external guests by default. The administrator wants to restrict guest invitations to only specific employees in the IT operations group. Which Microsoft Entra configuration accomplishes this?
options: Configure a Conditional Access policy that blocks the Microsoft Graph API for users not in the IT operations group, preventing the invitation API from being called by unauthorized senders
Configure a lifecycle workflow that runs on each guest invitation event, checks the inviter against the IT operations group, and revokes the invitation if the inviter is not a member
In External Identities, modify the External collaboration settings so guest invite permissions are restricted to a specific role or scope, such as the IT operations group via role assignment
In Privileged Identity Management, make the Guest Inviter role eligible-only for all users, then add the IT operations group as approvers of every guest invitation activation request
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because broadly blocking Graph for users would break far more than guest invitations and is not the right control. Option B is wrong because lifecycle workflows are joiner/mover/leaver focused and don't intercept invitations. Option C is correct because External collaboration settings in External Identities directly control who can invite guests, including limiting it to a specific role or group. Option D is wrong because PIM governs role activation, not who can call the invitation API.

questions.question: An organization wants partner users from another Microsoft Entra tenant to access shared Microsoft Teams channels and resources without going through the typical guest invitation and account redemption flow — partner users should use their home tenant identity directly. Which Entra capability fits?
options: Microsoft Entra Connect with password hash synchronization, which copies password hashes from the partner Active Directory into your tenant so partner users can sign in directly
Microsoft Entra B2B direct connect, which sets up a mutual trust between two Entra tenants so users from a remote tenant access shared Teams resources using their home identity
Microsoft Entra External ID for customers, which provides a separate consumer-focused tenant where partner users self-register and access shared workforce resources via user flows
Microsoft Entra cross-tenant synchronization, which provisions full member accounts from a partner tenant into your tenant using the SCIM-based provisioning engine for ongoing lifecycle
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because Entra Connect is for hybrid on-premises identity, not partner tenant trust. Option B is correct because B2B direct connect creates a mutual tenant-to-tenant trust so users access shared Teams resources with their home identity, without guest user accounts. Option C is wrong because External ID for customers is for consumer-facing apps, not partner workforce collaboration. Option D is wrong because cross-tenant sync provisions accounts as B2B guests in the target tenant — it is not trust-based shared access without guest accounts.

questions.question: A security analyst sees that Microsoft Entra ID Protection has flagged a user as high user risk based on detections such as leaked credentials and atypical travel. The organization wants high-risk users to be automatically required to reset their password the next time they sign in. Which combination fits?
options: Use a Microsoft Entra access review targeting high-risk users with a one-day recurrence and self-review with justification, automatically removing access if the user doesn't reset their password
Use Privileged Identity Management to make the user eligible for their own assigned roles and require MFA-on-activation; activation fails until the user resets their password via SSPR
Use lifecycle workflows in Entra ID Governance to trigger on user risk detections and run the leaver scenario, disabling the account and revoking licenses until an admin re-enables it
Use a Conditional Access policy that targets users with high user risk and applies the 'require password change' grant control, which forces secure password reset via SSPR on next sign-in
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because access reviews are about recertifying access, not remediating risk. Option B is wrong because PIM governs role activation, not user-risk-driven password change. Option C is wrong because lifecycle workflows don't react to risk detections; user risk integrates with Conditional Access. Option D is correct because a Conditional Access user-risk policy with 'require password change' is the standard automated remediation for high user risk via SSPR.

questions.question: A bank wants sign-ins to its corporate Microsoft 365 environment to be automatically blocked when Microsoft Entra ID Protection classifies the sign-in as high risk based on signals like anonymous IP, malware-linked IP, and unfamiliar sign-in properties. Which Conditional Access policy type fits?
options: A Conditional Access policy with a session control of sign-in frequency every hour, prompting the user to reauthenticate when Entra ID Protection raises new risk-relevant signals during sign-in
An Entra terms of use policy assigned through Conditional Access that requires re-accepting terms each time Entra ID Protection raises a high sign-in risk score for the current authentication
A Privileged Identity Management policy that converts standing access to eligible-only on first high-risk sign-in detection and requires re-activation with MFA-on-activation for further use
A Conditional Access policy with the sign-in risk condition set to High and the grant control set to Block access, preventing sign-ins flagged as high-risk by Entra ID Protection from succeeding
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because sign-in frequency reauthentication doesn't block — it just reprompts the user. Option B is wrong because terms of use display a disclaimer; they don't block based on risk. Option C is wrong because PIM is for role activation governance, not sign-in remediation. Option D is correct because the standard high-sign-in-risk remediation pattern is a Conditional Access sign-in-risk policy configured to block access.

questions.question: A startup is choosing between Microsoft Entra B2B collaboration and Microsoft Entra External ID for customers for a new use case where end users — millions of paying consumers — will sign in to a public web app. Which statement best distinguishes when to use Entra External ID for customers rather than B2B?
options: Use External ID for customers when end users are external consumers of your app and need a separate tenant with branded user flows, social IdP integration, and isolation from your workforce tenant
Use External ID for customers when partner employees need to access internal SharePoint sites and Teams using their home work credentials, since it provides direct shared-resource access for partners
Use External ID for customers when you need temporary just-in-time activation of administrative roles for external contractors helping support a consumer-facing app from a partner organization
Use External ID for customers when you need to invite a small number of partner users as guests with auto-redemption, where each user appears in your workforce tenant as a guest user object
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because External ID for customers is purpose-built for consumer-facing scenarios with a separate tenant, custom user flows, social IdPs, and isolation from the workforce tenant. Option B is wrong because partner workforce access to shared resources is B2B (collaboration or direct connect), not External ID for customers. Option C is wrong because contractor role activation is a PIM scenario in your workforce tenant. Option D is wrong because inviting a few partner guests is B2B collaboration, not External ID for customers.

questions.question: An administrator wants to allow guest users from a specific trusted partner tenant to sign in without performing MFA in their home tenant — because the partner enforces phishing-resistant MFA — and to trust those MFA claims when guests access the admin's tenant. Which B2B configuration fits?
options: Configure inbound cross-tenant access settings to trust multifactor authentication from the partner Entra tenant, so MFA claims issued by the partner's home tenant are honored for inbound guests
Configure a Conditional Access policy in the partner tenant that excludes your tenant from MFA enforcement, so guests are bypassed for MFA when they sign in to your tenant from home
Configure Microsoft Entra terms of use to display a partner-specific disclaimer that suppresses MFA prompts for guests who acknowledge that their home tenant already enforced multifactor auth
Configure a Privileged Identity Management policy that exempts guest users from the partner tenant from MFA-on-activation, since their home tenant's MFA claim provides proof of authentication
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because inbound cross-tenant access settings include the option to trust MFA, device compliance, and Entra hybrid joined claims from a specified partner tenant. Option B is wrong because exclusions in the partner's policy do not control trust in your tenant. Option C is wrong because terms of use are disclaimers, not MFA trust mechanisms. Option D is wrong because PIM governs role activation, not B2B authentication claims.

questions.question: A central IT team must onboard hundreds of suppliers' staff to a shared portal each month. Suppliers should self-request access via a catalog, choose from available bundles, route requests to an internal approver, and have access expire after the engagement. Which Entra ID Governance feature fits?
options: Microsoft Entra Entitlement Management with access packages organized in catalogs, allowing internal or external users to request bundles with approver workflows and automatic assignment expiration
Privileged Identity Management for groups, which lets supplier staff become eligible members of a security group and request just-in-time activation with MFA, justification, approval, and duration
Microsoft Entra Connect with provisioning rules that create supplier accounts based on attribute changes in a partner AD, then grant catalog access and expire it via lifecycle workflow leaver tasks
Microsoft Entra terms of use with custom acceptance pages for each supplier organization, tied to Conditional Access policies that grant temporary application access after acceptance and revoke later
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because Entitlement Management's access packages and catalogs provide exactly this self-service request, approver workflow, and automatic expiration experience for internal and external users. Option B is wrong because PIM for groups is for role-like just-in-time activation, not self-service supplier onboarding via a catalog. Option C is wrong because Entra Connect handles internal AD hybrid identity, not external supplier catalogs. Option D is wrong because terms of use are acceptance disclaimers, not bundled-access catalogs with approver workflows.

questions.question: An organization uses Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management and wants to receive automatic notifications when more than a defined number of users hold the Global Administrator role permanently active (rather than eligible). Which PIM capability supports this monitoring requirement?
options: Configure a Microsoft Entra access review of Global Administrator that runs daily with system as reviewer and counts permanent assignments before applying any auto-removal decisions to accounts
Configure a PIM alert such as 'There are too many administrators assigned to a role' or 'Roles are being assigned outside of PIM' to notify when standing administrator counts exceed thresholds
Configure Microsoft Entra ID Protection user-risk policies targeted at Global Administrator, which evaluate risky behavior including over-permissioned standing assignments and prompt for MFA
Configure a Conditional Access policy that requires administrative consent each time a Global Administrator role assignment is created, blocking direct assignment outside PIM activation workflows
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because access reviews don't fire threshold alerts; they recertify access. Option B is correct because PIM provides built-in alerts including 'too many administrators' and 'roles assigned outside PIM' that notify when standing privilege counts exceed thresholds. Option C is wrong because ID Protection evaluates per-user sign-in or user risk, not role assignment counts. Option D is wrong because Conditional Access cannot evaluate or gate role assignment creation actions.

questions.question: A federal contractor wants Microsoft Entra access reviews to require every reviewer to provide a written justification next to their approve or deny decision, so auditors can later inspect the rationale behind every recertification outcome. Which access review setting addresses this?
options: Configure the access review's recurrence to weekly with a one-day response window, so reviewers must respond quickly and the audit trail captures justification metadata from system signals
Configure the access review to use Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management as the reviewer, which records justifications captured during eligible role activation in the Entra audit logs
Configure Microsoft Entra terms of use to display the justification text as a disclaimer at the start of each review session, requiring reviewers to acknowledge their responsibilities first
Enable the 'Require reason on approval' (justification required) setting in the access review configuration, which forces each reviewer to enter a free-text justification for every decision
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because recurrence and response window control timing, not whether justification is required. Option B is wrong because PIM activation justifications are unrelated to access review decisions. Option C is wrong because terms of use display disclaimers; they don't capture per-decision justification text. Option D is correct because access reviews have a 'Require reason on approval' (justification required) setting that enforces a written reason for each decision.

questions.question: A security team is configuring an access package in Microsoft Entra Entitlement Management so an external partner can request access to a SharePoint site. They want the access package to be visible only to verified users from the partner's domain. Which Entitlement Management capability fits?
options: Configure the access package policy to allow requests from 'specific connected organizations' (or specific external users), so only users from the verified partner can see and request the package
Configure Microsoft Entra B2B direct connect for the partner tenant so the SharePoint site is shared through tenant-to-tenant trust, bypassing the need to publish an access package for partners
Configure a Conditional Access policy on the SharePoint site that requires the user's domain claim to match the partner's verified domain, blocking other guests from listing it in MyAccess
Configure Microsoft Entra terms of use for the SharePoint site to be visible only to the partner's domain, so the access package inherits the visibility scope of the associated terms of use
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because Entitlement Management policies can scope requestors to specific connected organizations or specific external users, controlling visibility to verified partners. Option B is wrong because direct connect doesn't use access packages and isn't a SharePoint sharing model with request workflows. Option C is wrong because Conditional Access policies don't filter access package visibility in MyAccess. Option D is wrong because terms of use are acceptance disclaimers, not visibility filters for access packages.

questions.question: An administrator wants Microsoft Entra to automatically execute tasks 7 days before a worker's last day, such as removing the user from all groups, revoking Microsoft 365 licenses, and disabling the account. The trigger should be the employee's leave date attribute. Which feature fits?
options: Microsoft Entra ID Governance lifecycle workflows configured for the leaver scenario, with a schedule offset relative to the user's employeeLeaveDateTime attribute and tasks like remove and disable
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management eligible role assignments with maximum activation duration set to 7 days, automatically deactivating roles and disabling the account at expiration
Microsoft Entra access reviews configured to run automatically 7 days before the user's leave date, using the system as reviewer and auto-apply outcomes that revoke licenses and group memberships
Microsoft Entra B2B cross-tenant synchronization configured for the leaver scenario, deprovisioning the user from partner tenants 7 days before the leave date and removing all license assignments
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because Entra ID Governance lifecycle workflows support attribute-based triggers (such as employeeLeaveDateTime) and the leaver tasks listed in the requirement. Option B is wrong because PIM is for role activation, not employee offboarding tasks. Option C is wrong because access reviews recertify access; they don't run scheduled leaver offboarding tasks. Option D is wrong because cross-tenant sync provisions identities to partner tenants; it doesn't run leaver tasks based on attribute triggers.

questions.question: An organization wants its Microsoft Entra B2B guest accounts to be automatically reviewed every quarter, with each guest required to self-attest that they still need access, and any guest who doesn't respond within the review window to be automatically removed from the tenant. Which feature fits?
options: Configure Privileged Identity Management for groups so all B2B guests become eligible members of a security group containing the resources, requiring quarterly activation with MFA and justification
Configure Entra ID Protection user-risk policies targeting B2B guests, which classify long-standing inactive guests as high risk and automatically revoke their accounts after a quarterly evaluation
Configure an access review of guest users in the tenant with quarterly recurrence, self-review by the guest, a defined response window, and auto-apply set to remove guests who don't respond in time
Configure cross-tenant access settings to expire any inbound B2B guest's redemption claim every 90 days, automatically deleting the guest's home realm discovery record and revoking redemption tokens
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because PIM for groups governs role-like activation, not periodic recertification of guest existence. Option B is wrong because ID Protection evaluates per-sign-in and per-user risk, not periodic attestation of need. Option C is correct because access reviews support reviewing guests, self-attestation, recurrence, response windows, and auto-apply removal. Option D is wrong because cross-tenant access settings govern trust and redemption rules, not periodic recertification.

questions.question: A governance administrator wants to ensure the same user cannot simultaneously hold both an 'Accounts Payable' access package and an 'Accounts Receivable' access package, because the combination creates an unacceptable separation-of-duties conflict. Which Entitlement Management feature fits?
options: Configure Privileged Identity Management active-only assignments for the Payable and Receivable groups so activating one role automatically suspends the eligibility of the other during the session
Configure a Conditional Access policy with a custom user attribute that flags users assigned to Payable as ineligible to sign in to the Receivable application, blocking the conflicting access
Configure separation-of-duties checks (incompatible access packages or groups) on the access package policies so a user already holding one package cannot request or be granted the other one
Configure Microsoft Entra terms of use with separate acceptance pages for Payable and Receivable, automatically revoking access to the other package whenever a user accepts terms for one of them
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because PIM does not bind incompatibility between two access packages in Entitlement Management. Option B is wrong because Conditional Access doesn't enforce separation of duties between access packages. Option C is correct because Entitlement Management policies support separation-of-duties checks that block users from receiving incompatible access packages or groups. Option D is wrong because terms of use are disclaimers, not SoD controls.

questions.question: Which statement most accurately describes the licensing requirement for Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management?
options: Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management requires Entra ID P2 licenses (or an equivalent suite such as Microsoft 365 E5) for users who are eligible, active, or approvers of PIM assignments
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management is included with the free tier of Entra ID and requires no additional licensing for users who activate eligible Entra roles or Azure resource roles
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management is included with Entra ID P1 for all users in the tenant, and only Global Administrators need an additional license to configure PIM policy settings
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management requires a separate Entra Suite license per tenant; individual user licenses are not required to enable just-in-time activation of administrative roles
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because PIM requires Entra ID P2 (or a suite that includes P2, such as Microsoft 365 E5) for the users who participate in PIM. Option B is wrong because PIM is not included in Entra ID Free. Option C is wrong because P1 does not include PIM; P1 covers Conditional Access and group-based access, while PIM requires P2. Option D is wrong because PIM is licensed per user via Entra ID P2, not as a tenant-wide PIM license.

questions.question: A security manager wants to confirm Microsoft Entra access reviews can be created against the assignees of a specific Entitlement Management access package, so recertification covers everyone who currently holds the package. Which statement most accurately describes the support for this?
options: Access reviews cannot target Entitlement Management access packages because access packages are managed entirely through requestor and approver policies, with their own built-in expiration model
Access reviews can target only Microsoft 365 groups and Entra security groups; recertification of access package assignees must be implemented manually with custom Microsoft Graph automation scripts

Access reviews can target the assignees of an Entitlement Management access package, in addition to groups, apps, and role assignments, supporting recurrence and auto-apply outcomes for unreviewed users

Access reviews can target access package assignees only when Privileged Identity Management is enabled in the tenant; otherwise the access package owner must run quarterly attestations manually in MyAccess
questions.explanation: Option A is wrong because access reviews do support access package assignees as a scope. Option B is wrong because access reviews aren't limited to M365 and security groups; they support apps, roles, and access packages. Option C is correct because access reviews can recertify assignees of an access package alongside groups, apps, and role assignments, with recurrence and auto-apply. Option D is wrong because access reviews for access packages don't require PIM.

questions.question: An organization uses Microsoft Entra ID Protection and wants to remediate medium-or-higher sign-in risk by requiring MFA at the time of sign-in instead of blocking access. Which Conditional Access pattern best matches this remediation goal?
options: Create a Conditional Access policy with user-risk set to High and grant control set to Block access, ensuring any high-risk user cannot sign in until an administrator resets their account
